title: danthonia pseudospicata
intro: Danthonia pseudospicata, also known as False Blue Oat Grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Danthonia pseudospicata is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusion, where the dried leaves and stems are steeped in hot water to create a tea.
